decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing Paradoxical Outcome to generate massive card advantage and create a powerful board state. By playing a mix of fast mana rocks like Black Lotus and Moxen, along with card draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Gitaxian Probe, this deck aims to quickly assemble a winning combo. Additionally, disruptive elements like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares help to protect the combo and disrupt opponents' game plans. With Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion, this deck can quickly recover from disruption and outvalue opponents in the late game.

The deck revolves around assembling the combo pieces needed to cast Doomsday and either win the game with Thassa's Oracle or control your opponent with Psychic Frog. The deck is built for speed and efficiency, with numerous card draw and tutor effects to find your combo pieces quickly. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds an additional layer of consistency to ensure you can execute your game plan effectively. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble your combo pieces to secure a victory.

The deck's primary objective is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to generate a significant advantage through card draw and mana ramp. By utilizing cards like Tinker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain along with powerful artifacts such as The One Ring and Coveted Jewel, the deck aims to control the game's pace and outvalue the opponent. With a mix of fast mana accelerants and disruptive elements like Force of Will, the deck can quickly establish a dominant position and overwhelm the opponent.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counter spells and removal, while also utilizing its own powerful spells and artifacts to gain card advantage and control the game. It relies on a mix of efficient creatures, powerful spells, and disruptive lands to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent, ultimately grinding them out of resources and overwhelming them with card advantage. The inclusion of Lurrus as a companion adds additional resiliency and recursion to the deck, allowing for even more control over the game state.

The deck focuses on using powerful artifacts and mana acceleration to enable a quick combo win condition centered around Coveted Jewel. By playing cards like Mishra's Workshop, Mana Crypt, and Moxen, the deck can quickly assemble the required mana to cast Coveted Jewel and activate its ability, allowing the player to take extra turns and gain a significant advantage over their opponent. Cards like Tinker, Time Vault, and Paradoxical Outcome help to further accelerate the combo and create a powerful board state that can quickly overwhelm the opponent.

The main idea of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan and control the tempo of the game through counterspells and removal spells. The deck aims to generate card advantage with powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Ponder, while also managing the opponent's threats with cards like Force of Will and Fatal Push. The inclusion of cards like Murktide Regent and Lórien Revealed provide additional win conditions for the deck, making it a versatile and powerful control archetype in Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this Combo-Dredge deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with key cards such as Narcomoeba, Ichorid, Prized Amalgam, and Golgari Grave-Troll using cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder. By utilizing cards like Creeping Chill and Silversmote Ghoul, the deck aims to drain the opponent's life total efficiently while creating a board presence with creatures like Hollow One and Grief. The deck also includes disruption elements like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void to control the opponent's strategy, allowing the combo pieces to come together smoothly.

The deck utilizes the Oath of Druids to quickly put powerful creatures into play while controlling the opponent's actions with counterspells and disruptive permanents like Null Rod. The deck also includes Show and Tell for additional cheating of creatures into play, as well as various tutors and fast mana to power out threats quickly. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with big threats like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and use control elements to protect them and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck revolves around quickly assembling a powerful board presence of artifact creatures and utilizing various artifacts to control the game. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler while also disrupting their strategy with cards like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle. By using cards like Manifold Key and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, the deck can turn small threats into big problems for the opponent. Additionally, the deck can generate mana quickly with Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb to power out its threats early in the game.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents by using hatebears and hate cards while also maintaining a fast and aggressive playstyle. By utilizing cards like Archon of Emeria and Void Mirror, the deck aims to slow down opponents' strategies while deploying efficient threats like Clarion Conqueror and Chancellor of the Annex. The combination of disruptive creatures and hate cards allows the deck to control the game's tempo and apply pressure on the opponent. With strong mana acceleration from cards like Chrome Mox and Ancient Tomb, the deck can quickly establish a board presence and overwhelm opponents.

This deck is focused on utilizing the companion ability of Lurrus of the Dream-Den to create a consistent aggro strategy. With a mix of efficient creatures like Dark Confidant, Guide of Souls, and Ocelot Pride, backed up by disruption spells such as Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares, this deck aims to apply early pressure while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the inclusion of artifacts like Black Lotus and Moxen provides fast mana acceleration, allowing for explosive plays. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a synergistic mix of creatures, disruption, and acceleration.

The deck focuses on using Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures into play, such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Oko, Thief of Crowns. It also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Thoughtseize to control the game. Additionally, the deck has cards like Show and Tell and Vampiric Tutor to set up game-winning plays. The mana base includes fetch lands and moxen for consistency and acceleration. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a dominant board presence with powerful creatures.

This deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful artifacts, focusing on generating mana efficiently and utilizing key artifacts like The One Ring, Time Vault, and Manifold Key to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes various mana acceleration cards like Grim Monolith and Mishra's Workshop to quickly build up a strong board presence. Additionally, cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void are included to disrupt the opponent's spells and slow down their game plan.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures and disruption spells to quickly take control of the game. It includes cards that disrupt opponent's strategies, such as Thoughtseize and Chalice of the Void, while also utilizing powerful creatures like Dark Confidant and Guide of Souls to apply pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes mana acceleration with cards like Black Lotus and the Moxes to get ahead early in the game. With a mix of disruption and aggressive threats, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory in Vintage 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on playing disruptive creatures early on to slow down the opponent's game plan, while also utilizing hate cards like Strip Mine, Chancellor of the Annex, and Void Mirror to disrupt their mana base and prevent them from casting key spells. The aggressive creatures like Archon of Emeria and Clarion Conqueror help to apply pressure and close out the game quickly. The deck relies on efficient mana acceleration from cards like Moxen and Ancient Tomb to get ahead on board and stay ahead. Overall, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while presenting a fast clock to win the game.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Coveted Jewel and utilize powerful combo pieces such as Tinker, Time Vault, and Paradoxical Outcome to generate immense value and card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Mishra's Workshop, Mystic Forge, and Urza's Saga allows for rapid mana acceleration and further manipulation of the deck. The presence of control elements like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor helps protect the combo setup and disrupt opponents' strategies. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm and outpace opponents with its potent synergy and combo potential.
